thesis: Apple Inc. designs consumer hardware and monetizes a global installed base through high-margin Services. **[Fact]** FY2025 consolidated net sales were **$307.0 billion**, operating income **$133.1 billion**, and net income **$112.0 billion** (diluted **$7.46** per share) per `AAPL/investor-documents/sec-edgar/10-K_20251031_rpt20250927_acc0000320193_25_000079.htm`. **[Fact]** FY2025 free cash flow was about **$98.8 billion** (**$6.58** per diluted share) after **$111.5 billion** of operating cash flow and **$12.7 billion** of capital spending.
why_market_wrong: The stock may trade as a perpetual quality compounder while hardware cycles and regulatory risk compress normalized free cash flow multiples. **[Inference]** Disaggregated Services net sales reached **$109.2 billion** in FY2025, yet the market may anchor on iPhone unit headlines rather than recurring revenue. **[Inference]** Proof-first component math at base case sums to about **$187** per share versus a price near **$327**, suggesting the market prices ecosystem durability beyond conservative filing-locked components.

key_assumptions.input: Starting free cash flow per share
key_assumptions.source: FY2025 OCF $111,482M less capex $12,715M ÷ ~15,015M diluted shares (AAPL/investor-documents/sec-edgar/10-K_20251031_rpt20250927_acc0000320193_25_000079.htm)
Mid-cycle iPhone refresh plus high-margin Services growth; modest buyback tailwind

open_questions: Confirm Services milestone band ($0–$85/sh) versus deferred revenue already in consolidated FCF.
China and antitrust reserve band (−$45 to −$8/sh) pending regulatory outcomes.
Live price and final base IRR after mechanical refresh.
